title: Interrupt-vector translation lookaside buffer
abstract: Systems, methods and apparatuses relating to processor cores that respond to interrupts are disclosed. In one embodiment, an apparatus includes an interrupt interface, a memory interface; and a processor core to generate an interrupt acknowledge signal in response to a received interrupt; receive data in return; determine whether the received data is an interrupt service routine address, the interrupt service routine address being stored in an interrupt vector translation lookaside buffer; and, if not, use the received data to calculate the interrupt service routine address; wherein the processor core is further to use the interrupt service routine address to issue a request on the memory interface to fetch the interrupt service routine, and to execute the interrupt service routine.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. An apparatus, comprising: an interrupt interface; a memory interface; and a processor core to generate an interrupt acknowledge signal in response to a received interrupt; receive data in return; determine whether the received data is an interrupt service routine address, the interrupt service routine address being stored in an interrupt vector translation lookaside buffer; and, if not, use the received data to calculate the interrupt service routine address; wherein the processor core is further to use the interrupt service routine address to issue a request on the memory interface to fetch the interrupt service routine, and to execute the interrupt service routine. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the received data is an interrupt vector. 3. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 and 2, wherein the processor core to retrieve and execute microcode from a microcode storage. 4. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 and 2, further comprising means for retrieving and executing microcode. 5. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 and 2, wherein the processor core to execute the microcode at a ring-0 privilege level. 6. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the received interrupt is selected from one of P interrupt numbers, and the interrupt vector translation lookaside buffer comprises a table with Q rows, each row comprising a valid bit and an address; wherein each of the valid bits is set to 0 after power-on or reset. 7. The apparatus of claim 6, wherein P equals Q, the table has one row corresponding to each of the P interrupt sources, and P can be used as an address to access the table. 8. The apparatus of claim 6, wherein P is larger than Q, and the processor core further to select a sacrificial row of the interrupt vector translation lookaside buffer to overwrite with the address and valid bit. 9. The apparatus of any one of claims 7 and 8, wherein each of the P sources of interrupts to route its interrupt to an interrupt controller, which is to generate the interrupt to the processor core. 10. The apparatus of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the processor core to determine whether the data is the interrupt service routine address by inspecting all but the lowest eight bits of the data, and, if any of those bits is non-zero, then the data comprises the address of the interrupt service routine. 11. The apparatus of claim 10, wherein the data comprises an interrupt number; and wherein the processor core further to walk a content-addressable memory containing an interrupt descriptor table and a content-addressable memory containing a global descriptor table to identify the address associated with the interrupt service routine for the interrupt number, and to use the address to issue a request on the memory interface to fetch the interrupt service routine from a memory, and to execute the interrupt service routine. 12. The apparatus of any one of claims 10 and 11, wherein the processor core further to write the address to the interrupt vector translation lookaside buffer and to set the valid bit at a row associated with the interrupt number. 13. An interrupt controller, comprising: an interrupt interface to connect to a processor core; an interrupt vector translation lookaside buffer to store a plurality of addresses and a plurality of associated valid bits; an execution circuit to: transmit an interrupt for an interrupt number to the processor core, receive an interrupt acknowledgement for the interrupt number from the processor core; send an interrupt vector corresponding to the interrupt number to the processor core, and, at substantially the same time, to send the interrupt number to a read address port of an interrupt vector translation lookaside buffer. 14. The interrupt controller of claim 13, wherein the interrupt vector translation lookaside buffer to comprise Q entries, with one entry for each of P interrupts. 15. The interrupt controller of claim 14, wherein P is larger than Q. 16. The interrupt controller of claim 15, wherein the execution circuit further to select a sacrificial row of the interrupt vector translation lookaside buffer when writing a value to it. 17. The interrupt controller of any one of claims 14 and 15, wherein P is larger than Q, and the processor core further to select a sacrificial row of the interrupt vector translation lookaside buffer to overwrite with the address and valid bit. 18. The interrupt controller of any one of claims 14 and 15, wherein the interrupt controller further to apply a read address equal to P to the interrupt vector translation lookaside buffer. 19. The interrupt controller of any one of claims 13 to 16, further comprising a selector to select the interrupt vector if an output of the interrupt vector translation lookaside buffer is invalid, and, otherwise, to select the output of the interrupt vector translation lookaside buffer. 20. A method of utilizing an interrupt vector translation lookaside buffer as part of an interrupt scheme, the interrupt vector translation lookaside buffer comprising Q entries, with one entry for each of P interrupts, each of the Q entries comprising a valid bit and an extended instruction pointer, the method comprising: resetting the valid bit of each of the Q entries to 0 at power-on, or reset, or both; setting the valid bit of a selected one of the Q entries of the interrupt vector translation lookaside buffer; setting the extended instruction pointer of the selected one of the Q entries of the interrupt vector translation lookaside buffer; and providing the extended instruction pointer of the selected one of the Q entries in response to a read request for the entry associated with the selected one of the Q entries and received by the interrupt vector translation lookaside buffer. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein P equals Q, the interrupt vector translation lookaside buffer has one row corresponding to each of the P interrupt sources, and P can be used as an address to access the buffer. 22. The method of claim 20, wherein P is larger than Q, further comprising selecting a sacrificial row of the interrupt vector translation lookaside buffer to overwrite with the extended instruction pointer and valid bit. 23. The method of any one of claims 20 and 21, wherein P equals Q, further comprising reading an entry at address P of the interrupt vector translation lookaside buffer at substantially the same time as sending the interrupt vector. 24. The method of any one of claims 20 to 22 further comprising each of the P interrupts routing its interrupt to an interrupt controller, and generating an interrupt by the interrupt controller to a processor. 25. A non-transitory machine readable medium including code, when executed, to cause a machine to perform the method of any one of claims 20-22.
